title: The Robot That Couldn't See Amara
description: Amara discovers that the school's AI tutor keeps skipping her — and goes on a mission to find out why.

steps.content: Amara is twelve and lives in Lagos. Her school just got a brand-new AI system that decides which students get extra tutoring. Every week, the system makes its list — but Amara and her friend Kwame are never on it, even though they're both struggling with fractions.

steps.question: Why might an AI system treat students from different places unfairly?
options: The AI is being deliberately mean
The AI was trained mostly on data from schools in other countries

steps.content: A visiting student researcher named Dr. Yemi helps them understand. She uses an analogy: imagine a cookbook written only for people who like spicy food. It wouldn't help people who don't — not because they're wrong, but because the book was never written with them in mind. The AI had only learned from schools in Europe and North America.

steps.instruction: Amara and Kwame want to prove the AI is unfair. What's the best way to gather evidence?
options: Interview 20 students and map who the AI recommends vs. who actually needs help
Complain to the teacher without any evidence

steps.content: They gathered the evidence, built a clear case, and presented it to the principal. The school paused the AI system pending an external review. Amara learned something important: questioning AI isn't anti-technology. It's good science. The best AI is built with everyone in mind.
